section_text_parts: PATCH is less commonly used and known than PUT .
And many teams use only PUT , even for partial updates.
You are free to use them however you want, FastAPI doesn't impose any restrictions.
But this guide shows you, more or less, how they are intended to be used.
tutorial/body-updates::section-0004::adm.section_text:
PATCH is less commonly used and known than PUT .

And many teams use only PUT , even for partial updates.

You are free to use them however you want, FastAPI doesn't impose any restrictions.

But this guide shows you, more or less, how they are intended to be used.

section_text_parts: You can actually use this same technique with an HTTP PUT operation.
But the example here uses PATCH because it was created for these use cases.
tutorial/body-updates::section-0007::adm.section_text:
You can actually use this same technique with an HTTP PUT operation.

But the example here uses PATCH because it was created for these use cases.

section_text_parts: Notice that the input model is still validated.
So, if you want to receive partial updates that can omit all the attributes, you need to have a model with all the attributes marked as optional (with default values or None ).
To distinguish from the models with all optional values for updates and models with required values for creation , you can use the ideas described in Extra Models .
tutorial/body-updates::section-0007::adm.section_text:
Notice that the input model is still validated.

So, if you want to receive partial updates that can omit all the attributes, you need to have a model with all the attributes marked as optional (with default values or None ).

To distinguish from the models with all optional values for updates and models with required values for creation , you can use the ideas described in Extra Models .
